description: Google Cloud Storage is Google's managed object storage service for storing and retrieving unstructured data in buckets and objects. It is used for application storage, archives, media delivery, analytics datasets, and similar object-storage workloads.
summary: storage.googleapis.com exposes Cloud Storage HTTP APIs (JSON and XML), a Cloud Storage MCP surface, and Google Cloud CLIs (`gcloud storage`, `gsutil`); OAuth 2.0 Google Cloud credentials are documented for the JSON API and CLIs, while XML API and MCP auth details were not fully pinned from the docs read. Public unauthenticated access is also supported for objects that bucket owners make public.
